programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a hotel frequented by foreign diplomats after a woman's body is found wrapped in bedsheets. They tangle with a trade representative whose murky past suggests he could be the killer. Meanwhile, Danny argues with his boss over the corpse of a homeless man found covered with silver paint. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Taylor investigates the murder of a student who has been beaten and shot, and the discovery of heroin traces in his apartment leads him to suspect the killing was drug-related. The case becomes more urgent when it emerges that the victim's female room-mate has disappeared. Stella embarks on a harsh line of interrogation that results in her methods being called into question

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A routine investigation at a crime scene turns sour when Danny is attacked by a criminal hiding in the victim's house. He gives chase to the subway, where shots are fired - during which the offender vanishes and a police officer is killed, leaving Danny as prime suspect. Meanwhile, Stella investigates the discovery of a nanny's body in a Central Park public lavatory. Drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A billionaire hires the team to help when his daughter is kidnapped. Meanwhile, Walter upsets the gang when he is interviewed and fails to acknowledge the other members. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team helps to facilitate secret peace talks to prevent a third world war when a conflict in Eastern Europe threatens to escalate. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team set up a stake-out at a storage facility to catch a thief suspected of selling hi-tech Navy equipment on the black market, but end up witnessing a murder. To make matters worse, $90million worth of radar gear goes missing while they are distracted. Meanwhile, Ducky asks Abby to run a series of mysterious blood tests, raising suspicions that he is harbouring a secret

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A suspect in a drug-smuggling case is seemingly mauled to death by his own dog, which is immediately scheduled to be destroyed - but Abby refuses to hand it over to Navy officials and launches her own investigation. Meanwhile, Gibbs continues to worry about Shepard's health, convinced she and Ducky are hiding something. Drama, starring Pauley Perrette, Mark Harmon and Lauren Holly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are investigated by the FBI on suspicion of murdering arms dealer La Grenouille. Jenny Shepard is the prime suspect, as she believed the criminal killed her father some years earlier, so Gibbs forms a plan to help his boss. However, an eyewitness later comes forward and implicates Tony. Starring Lauren Holly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Marine is seemingly killed in a mortar attack on America's heavily fortified administrative HQ in Baghdad, but a subsequent autopsy reveals he was murdered. Intelligence analyst Nikki Jardine volunteers to help Tony investigate the case - but her behaviour in Iraq makes him suspect she had an ulterior motive for requesting the assignment

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A dramatic undercover operation results in the death of a serial killer, seemingly bringing the case to an abrupt conclusion. However, it gradually becomes clear that he may have worked with an accomplice - and evidence emerges to implicate the new man in Ziva's life. Drama, starring Cote de Pablo and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A billionaire hires the team to help when his daughter is kidnapped. Meanwhile, Walter upsets the gang when he is interviewed and fails to acknowledge the other members. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.desc: The team helps to facilitate secret peace talks to prevent a third world war when a conflict in Eastern Europe threatens to escalate. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs sends DiNozzo and Ziva to retrieve top-secret information from a secure military base - a mission aimed at catching a mole in the NCIS ranks. Unfortunately, it soon becomes clear that the traitor is still at large - so the building is locked down and all agents are rounded up in a final effort to catch the culprit. Guest starring Jude Ciccolella

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Having identified the mole in their ranks, the agents arrest the traitor's contact Ted Bankston, a former NSA agent working for a kidnapper code-named the Weatherman - who is making millions on the stock markets out of stolen military data. Drama, with Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed in what initially seems to be a road-rage incident - until investigations reveal evidence of foul play. Digging deeper, the team finds the dead man belonged to an underground fight club, and sets about discovering whether his involvement was a factor in the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, the team works overtime to solve a robbery - a case made doubly difficult by the fact that the prime suspect is a former petty officer who has supposedly been dead for 17 years. Eventually arrested at a Vietnam memorial, he manages to convince Gibbs of his innocence - but a discovery in the forensics lab suggests he may be guilty after all. Guest starring Peter Coyote

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee is sent to a women's prison to interview a murder suspect, but ends up being captured by the inmates, who have run riot and seized control of a cell block, afraid they will be blamed for killing a guard. Held hostage as a bargaining chip, he tries to identify the real culprit before the warden takes drastic action to resolve the crisis

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Missiles are stolen from the SeaTac base, and later turn up in a car belonging to a military weapons expert. Rabb and Austin are quick to pinpoint a suspect - but a prison break-out soon complicates matters. David James Elliott stars

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A murder suspect's claims of innocence shake Mac's confidence and make him doubt the validity of prosecution evidence. Meanwhile, a link emerges between the cases of a scantily clad woman hit by a lorry and a baseball fan who died after the defeat of his favourite team. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac gets caught in the middle of a shooting at a cafe while having breakfast, and finds himself torn between whether to pursue the gunman or help a waitress injured in the attack. As the team gives chase, he is presented with more personal decisions to make. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the case of a skyscraper climber who plummeted from the Empire State Building. Returning to the floor from which he fell, Mac and Flack discover the dead man may have witnessed a murder. Meanwhile, Stella and Danny look into the killing of a fashion designer

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members' helicopter crashes while they are out on a mission to rescue a group of lost hikers, and the crash ignites a wildfire, which soon surrounds them. Meanwhile, Sylvester tries to help the injured pilot who is unable to make it to safety. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team protects a key witness who is en route to testify against a deadly drug cartel by taking her on the run - but the criminals are hot on their heels. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the scene of a fatal car crash involving marine lance corporal Chad Dunn and student Lyla Cutwright. Duckie notices that the woman's injuries do not match the specifics of the accident and there are no skidmarks found at the scene, leading Gibbs to declare they possibly have a murder and a suicide on their hands. Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the case of a marine who dropped dead while scaling a wall on an assault course, and a fellow soldier reveals that he was heavily involved with an underground fight club, where competitors would battle for cash. Meanwhile, having returned to work after compassionate leave, Vance discovers paperwork and a key in his late wife's effects that lead him to a safety deposit box containing a stash of cash

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While transporting the corpse of Lt Gordon Roth to the morgue, a flat tyre brings Ducky and Jimmy to a halt and they are abducted and taken to a remote cabin - where one of their captors wants the post-mortem to take place. Back at the scene, Abby finds a blood sample that does not belong to the victim, who in fact bore little resemblance to the picture of Roth in his old school yearbook. Guest starring Valerie Cruz (The Following)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members' helicopter crashes while they are out on a mission to rescue a group of lost hikers, and the crash ignites a wildfire, which soon surrounds them. programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team protects a key witness who is en route to testify against a deadly drug cartel by taking her on the run - but the criminals are hot on their heels. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky is assaulted by a man who claims to have previously undisclosed information about his half-brother, who died decades ago. While his NCIS colleagues traces the attacker's whereabouts, Dr Mallard recalls the emotional final days he spent with his sibling. Elsewhere, as the holiday season approaches, Bishop and Jake discuss the future of their relationship. Naval crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The first instalment of a two-part crossover with NCIS: New Orleans sees the Washington, DC team co-operating with their colleagues from the Big Easy to investigate a spate of lethal poisonings on board a New Orleans-to-DC flight. All the evidence seems to point to missing chef Luca, who happens to be Abby's brother, so the two investigative services join forces to locate him, and find out why a private tech firm seems to have been targetted by the killer. Naval crime drama, guest starring Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: The concluding instalment of a two-part crossover with the original NCIS series sees Sebastian joining Abby Sciuto in Washington, DC to pick through the forensic evidence relating to the aircraft murders. Meanwhile, DC agent Ellie Bishop heads to New Orleans to help Pride and his team track down the chief suspect - Abby's brother Luca. Naval crime drama, guest starring Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects foul play when a seaman who was set to testify before a grand jury dies before he can make his court appearance. When it becomes clear the man was murdered, the subsequent investigation reveals that a previously thwarted human-trafficking ring has been re-established. Meanwhile, a storm causes a massive power outage, and McGee seeks a room for the night among his colleagues. Naval crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A deep-sea diver is murdered on the job, and his co-workers all become suspects. However, when they return to the surface, they must remain in a high-tech decompression chamber for four days. With each of their suspects confined within a tank, the NCIS team must complete an unorthodox investigation from the outside. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

Swashbuckling adventure, featuring Downton Abbey creator Julian Fellowes and starring Sean Bean and Brian Cox

programme.desc: Part two of two. The amateur sleuths continue their investigation after Martin Frazer's cousin, who supposedly drowned, is spotted alive and well a few days later. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Disaster strikes when Mike's favourite country musician Holly Collins is found electrocuted in what appears to be a drunken accident or possibly suicide. Police drama set in the 1950s, starring Jennie Jacques and Kieran Bew

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: In an episode originally screened in 1992, the long suffering Scottish detective tries to defend a childhood friend recently released from prison when a series of shootings puts him in the frame for murder. Mark McManus stars as gruff crime buster Jim Taggart, while series regulars Blythe Duff (DS Jackie Reid) and James MacPherson (Mike Jardine) also star. There's also an appearance from Clare Grogan, best known for her work with the band Altered Images and cult TV hit Red Dwarf

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The Glaswegian detective (Mark McManus) and sidekick Jardine investigate the latest in a long line of murders by a calculating killer known only as the Mechanic. Feature-length episode of the police drama, starring Mark McManus, James Macpherson, Sian Thomas and John Bluthal

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: New recruit Sergeant Callum Stone (Sam Callis) makes an instant impact at Sun Hill by subduing a violent drunk in the station, but his maverick methods instantly alarm Dan and raise Smithy's hackles. Terry traces a series of threatening calls made to elderly women back to a single suspect, a supervisor at a kitchen fitting company, who is less than pleased to be arrested in a lift stalled between floors. Michael Angelis guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Following a report of vandalism on a church, Terry finds himself investigating a case of assisted suicide in which conflicting evidence from the grieving family members suggests someone has something to hide. Meanwhile, Beth and Roger are called to a pet shop where the owner has been tied up and several dogs let loose, but it's Sun Hill's own bloodhound Callum Stone who realises there is something more sinister than youthful high-jinks going on

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A woman is robbed of her winnings on the way home from a casino, and suspicion soon falls on the barman who called her a taxi - which was driven by his friend. The case against the men grows stronger when it turns out they once before arranged transport for a woman who was attacked, so Emma Keane is sent undercover as a casino punter - only for events to take a surprising turn. Michael Brandon guest stars

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Four-part adaptation of Charlotte Bronte's novel about an orphan girl who struggles to survive the harsh treatment of a boarding school to embark on a career as a governess. Jane takes up her first position at the home of the alluring and unpredictable Edward Rochester, under the watchful eye of laundrywoman Grace Poole. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Jane begins to wonder about the chemistry between herself and Rochester, who disappoints her when he leaves without warning. However, he returns with a houseful of distinguished guests, including the beautiful Blanche Ingram and her opinionated mother. Things turn sour when a mysterious visitor arrives. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: After fulfilling her dying aunt's last wishes, Jane returns to Thornfield Hall - only to find Mr Rochester is set to wed Blanche. However, after she confesses her love, he shares his true feelings and asks for her hand in marriage instead. While all seems well at first, a ghostly figure begins to torment Jane as the wedding day draws near. Drama, with Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: A clergyman and his two sisters take a heartbroken Jane under their wing after finding her alone and starving on the moors. The governess embarks on a new life as a parish teacher, but thinks she hears Rochester call out her name and feels compelled to return to Thornfield - where the secrets of the North Tower are finally revealed. Starring Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Fifteen Streets
programme.desc: Feature-length drama based on Catherine Cookson's novel set in early 20th-century Newcastle. Worker John O'Brien lives near poverty in a small house in the workers district, yet falls in love with teacher Mary, daughter of a rich family. Despite the pressures exerted by the social difference, the vigorous Mary refuses to allow outer circumstances destroy their relationship. Starring Sean Bean and Clare Holman

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives are busy preparing for Sister Evangelina's surprise jubilee celebrations, marking 50 years since she took her vows, but a face from the past threatens to ruin the party for the guest of honour. It's back to business when a young woman with Down's syndrome is brought in feeling unwell. To everyone's surprise, they discover she is six months pregnant, causing some of them to think the worst, and the staff are joined by new nurse Patsy, whose sense of humour and no-nonsense attitude make her an instant hit

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie is chuffed when local curate Tom invites her to join him for a day of cricket, sending her to work with a spring in her step. Is love on the cards for the young midwife? Shelagh's infertility has made her think long and hard about what she wants in life, so she approaches her husband with an idea, while Patsy upsets a young mother in the clinic and is moved to the district rota, where she works tirelessly to diagnose a docker's unusual symptoms

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A body is discovered floating in the pool of an exclusive cosmetic surgery clinic and several things point to suicide - the victim was going through a messy divorce and there is an empty pill box in her room. But DI Poole has several reasons to believe Valerie Dupree was murdered - not least the fact she poured herself a cup of the world's most expensive tea but only drank half. The pressure is on to find the culprit as the clinic is highly respected on the island and the chief surgeon is an old friend of Commissioner Patterson - and as the detectives begin to dig, they discover many sordid secrets among the guests and staff. Cherie Lunghi (Secret Diary of a Call Girl), James Fleet (The Vicar of Dibley) and Emma Pierson (Hotel Babylon) guest star

programme.desc: The body of a woman is found on train tracks in London, badly damaged from electrical burns, and the post-mortem stirs up troubling memories from Nikki's childhood

Meanwhile, the net closes in on Ellen as Harry demands a full investigation

programme.desc: Carol makes an important decision about getting married. Pauline gets a shock and Mark struggles to seek the truth about his dad's imprisonment

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The store holds a special week promoting German goods, and Captain Peacock boosts his authority by donning lederhosen. However, an influx of Teutonic tourists puts paid to the staff's hopes of a sales boom. Shop-floor comedy, guest starring Joanna Lumley, with Trevor Bannister and John Inman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Pilot episode of Roy Clarke's long-running comedy. Two men steal Compo's TV and he retaliates by removing Nora Batty's underwear from her washing line. Bill Owen stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The boys discuss life, love and women - particularly the unpleasant ones working in the cafe. Later, Compo realises he has lost his house key. Michael Bates, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A middle-class couple's adopted child is kidnapped, a case that takes Gently and Bacchus to a home where unmarried mothers are forced to give up their babies. Suspicion initially falls upon the natural mother, who is thought to have 'stolen' her baby back. But further investigations reveal a much darker side to the adoption home itself and raise questions as to how far this seemingly perfect couple were prepared to go to become parents. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Mark Gatiss, Helen Baxendale and Alison Steadman

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The daughter of a man held responsible for a fatal boating accident on the Thames comes forward with new evidence, claiming that a property developer sabotaged the vessel. The case means putting Strickland's wealthy sailing friends under the spotlight, much to the team's delight. Guest starring Barbara Flynn (Cranford)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A recently released convict comes under suspicion when a warden at his parole hostel is murdered, but when the cold case squad becomes involved, it transpires the former prisoner may have been the intended target rather than the perpetrator, and that this killing may be connected to previous, unsolved crimes. Nigel Terry and Kerry Fox guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Neil is badly let down by one of his regular informants when a family jewellers is robbed of half a million pounds' worth of stock and one of the partners is shot in the course of the crime. Phil plays the knight in shining armour for casino owner's wife Monica Dreyfuss when she turns up in custody drunk and unable to pay her restaurant bill

programme.title: Bramwell

programme.title: Norfolk (2015)
programme.desc: A mercenary leads an idyllic life with his son in a rural environment, until the day a mysterious stranger arrives bearing instructions. He must carry out one last mission - the assassination of a group of revolutionaries - but the assignment threatens to destroy his bond with his child. Drama, starring Denis Menochet and Barry Keoghan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the fourth round ties, including Cheltenham Town v Manchester City at Jonny-Rocks Stadium and West Ham United v Doncaster Rovers at London Stadium. Cheltenham and Doncaster of Leagues Two and One respectively were looking to produce upsets against top-flight opposition and progress to the last 16

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Luton Town (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the encounter at Stamford Bridge. Luton defeated fellow Championship side 1-0 in the previous round and will be looking to demonstrate how much they have progressed having been playing non-league football as recently at the 2013/14 season. However, the Blues, who swept aside Morecambe 4-0 in the previous round, have great pedigree in this competition, winning it eight times with the most recent of those triumphs coming in 2018

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team find four tip-bound treasures found in Greater Manchester and the West Midlands, and transform them into profitable pieces

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how the uneven distribution of freshwater around the globe has given rise to an incredible diversity of species and habits, from the driest desert to the lushest tropical rainforest. Featuring a colony of ants banding together into a raft every time its home in the Amazon floods, a rain frog that manages to eke out an existence in one of the world's driest habitats on Earth, and the last wild camels that survive the Gobi Desert's bitter winters by eating snow that blows in from Siberia

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v Liverpool (Kick-off 5.00pm). All the action from the clash between the rivals at Old Trafford. The sides last met in the Premier League a week ago at Anfield, while the last meeting between them in this competition came at this stage of the 2011/12 campaign, when Liverpool recorded a 2-1 home win thanks to a late winner by Dirk Kuyt

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is staying close to home exploring the Chilterns landscape and the people and skills that have shaped this Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. He discovers some hidden historical gems revealed by a revolutionary new mapping technique, heads deep into the woods to learn about the ancient art of bodging and rolls his sleeves up to help clean up ponds which are so desperately needed by wildlife. Joe Crowley investigates if large- scale schemes such as HS2 tally with the Government's pledge to go green, while Adam Henson calls in a horse whisperer to help with a nervous Exmoor pony

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how Earth's five oceans connect to form the largest ecosystem on the planet, and how its network of currents supports the health of the seas and marine wildlife. Cameras follow vast super-pods of dolphins and the flocks of gannets that follow them as they scour the oceans searching for food, as well as the tiny yet dazzling flamboyant cuttlefish that's looking for a mate

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: With the police operation pending, a nervous waiting game ensues for Charles to return to Kanit House with Marie-Andree and Ajay, but he might be one step ahead of the situation. Later, as a clock ticks down, Herman and Charles are locked in an exhausting battle of wits

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from today's fourth-round ties, including Manchester United v Liverpool at Old Trafford and Brentford v Leicester City at Brentford Community Stadium. Fierce rivals United and Liverpool met for the second week in succession, while the clash between Leicester and the Bees featured two teams who had impressed in their respective leagues so far this term. Plus, Chelsea v Luton Town, Fulham v Burnley and Everton v Sheffield Wednesday

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury is joined by Shelley Kerr to present action from the weekend's Super League fixtures, including Arsenal v West Ham United and Tottenham Hotspur v Everton

programme.title: Election (1999)
programme.desc: A teacher develops an irrational dislike for an overambitious student and plans to sabotage her campaign for school president. He persuades a dim-witted athlete to stand, in the hope he will pip the high-achieving front-runner to the post - but the intervention of another student throws the plan into disarray. Satirical comedy, starring Matthew Broderick, Reese Witherspoon, Chris Klein and Jessica Campbell

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones return with the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell check out a listed cottage in Canterbury, a three-bed semi in Bryncethin, South Wales, and a piece of land in Yardley, Birmingham

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Persephone and Debbie Santa Clara de Magdalene, who sampled life in New Zealand, hoping it might just be the country for them and their children

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood returns with the programmes that reveals how the police and the public are using CCTV evidence and technology to catch criminals in the act. A specialist crime squad stop a mugger on a moped in his tracks, intruders prowl the tiny streets of a model village in the dead of night, and insurance cheats are brought to justice when their fake falls are exposed

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Danny Sebastian help two teams look for items in Shrewsbury to sell at an auction in East Yorkshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp has a mystery for fair-goers to solve

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest walks in on a man standing over the body of his dead wife, while Mrs McCarthy strikes it lucky at the church bingo with the help of a fortune teller her sister has befriended. Guest starring Niamh Cusack

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers bike from north to south through Europe, searching for the best baking each country has to offer. They start their 5,000-mile trek in Norway, picking up tips and recipes from professionals and home bakers

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple from Bedfordshire who are looking for a character property in Norfolk with a good-sized garden for some new canine companions, all on their budget of £450,000. Ginny also heads to Great Yarmouth, where she straps in for a white-knuckle ride on one of the country's last remaining wooden rollercoasters

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Elwyn and Ann-Sophie take their large range of vegetables to Cowbridge Farmers' Market in South Wales, where pig farmers Liz and Andrew also sell their charcuterie

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a spice box, two Chinese statues, a funfair horse, a 1900s cabinet and a dummy called Steve. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy worries when she cannot find Ian and questions Sharon. Lexi is unwell at school, so Ben asks Callum to collect her, only to be told he cannot take her home as he is not family. Alarm bells ring for Linda when she accompanies Frankie to the crematorium on the anniversary of her little brother Harry's death. Chelsea reassures Denise she has not been in touch with Lucas, but tells him he is a changed man

programme.title: Katie Price: Harvey and Me
programme.desc: Model and businesswoman Katie Price's son Harvey was born with a rare disorder that affects brain function, hormones and vision, and he has a long list of medical and behavioural conditions that mean his future is unpredictable. This documentary follows Katie and Harvey as he turns 18, with the pair exploring the options of further education colleges and meeting other families that contain disabled young adults

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: David and Michael are both upset when they discover who the studio has now cast as they each have a history with the actors taking their place - one of whom is known and the other unknown. Comedy, starring David Tennant and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Accepting their fate is to no longer be involved in the American project, David and Michael prepare to finally leave their homes. David is finally due to go back to work, Michael thinks he finally has a route to New York, and the girls perform their charity sketch

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The singing, acting and dancing skills of the contestants are tested in Rats - The Rusical, the programme's first- ever live musical. Guest judge and West End star Sheridan Smith joins Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host invites award-winning actress Carey Mulligan to talk about her role in the revenge thriller Promising Young Woman. Hollywood and Broadway star Neil Patrick Harris also guests, discussing his appearance in Russell T Davies' 1980s-set serial It's a Sin. Plus, ex-footballer Ian Wright promotes his podcast is Ian Wright's Everyday People, Grantchester star James Norton reflects on his role in the powerful drama Nowhere Special and French actress Camille Cottin also guests. Singer-songwriter Celeste performs her current single Love Is Back

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. Now, clinical psychologist Professor Tanya Byron joins former England footballer Alex Scott to discover how the latest science can help us gain greater control over our state of mind and improve mental health and wellbeing

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood share some eerie events from the natural world, including a dust storm which turned an entire city red, and a cloud shaped like a UFO

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC friends explore a bamboo forest in China

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a den for 11-year-old aspiring journalist Soibi, who is finding it hard to write up her reports in the small bedroom she shares with her three-year-old sister

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: The enthusiastic young cook creates three new popcorn flavours for his family's movie night

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Scottish Bake Off champion Peter Sawkins joins the team, cooking up a treat to celebrate Burns Night

programme.title: Big Cats About the House
programme.desc: Following the work of big cat expert Giles Clark, as he embarks on a mission to transform the Big Cat Sanctuary in Kent into a centre of global excellence for conservation. In the opening episode, Giles provides around the clock care to Maya, a five-day-old black jaguar cub neglected by her mother. Then, he travels to Cambodia to witness the dilemma clouded leopards are facing in the wild

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as four more professional dog groomers are put to the test. First up in the Breed Makeover round are four English cocker spaniels, as the contestants try to tame the gun dogs' wig and furnishings. They are then challenged to create a tricky spiral around the coats of four frizzy-haired models, before parading them on the dogwalk, where their owners see the results for the first time. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don heads to India, where he takes in some of the most masterful examples of horticultural and architectural extravagance in the world - including the gardens of the Taj Mahal. Along the way, the green-fingered presenter investigates the majestic tomb gardens of the Mughal emperors, and a unique rock sculpture garden created from discarded objects and rubbish

programme.title: Fast Girls (2012)
programme.desc: A fierce rivalry develops between a talented working-class sprinter and the wealthy daughter of a former Olympic medal-winner as they compete on the track. They are forced to settle their differences and work together when they are recruited for the same relay team. Drama, with Lily James, Lenora Crichlow, Rupert Graves and Noel Clarke, who also co-wrote the script

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the first open singles semi-final. Eventual champion Robert Paxton recorded a dominant 8-5, 10-1 victory over Les Gillett at this stage last year

programme.title: A Cook Abroad
programme.desc: The MasterChef: The Professionals judge embarks on a culinary road trip through the Jura region of eastern France, spending time with the area's food producers to see how their passion translates into world-class ingredients. She joins a boar hunt and faces up to the responsibility of eating meat, learns the secret of making some of the UK's favourite French cheeses and discovers an ancient profession she never knew existed. Finally, on the slopes of Mont d'Or, Monica prepares a rustic feast using produce she gathered herself

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: For six years zoological and marine mammal reproductive physiologist Holley Muraco has been trying without success to breed Pacific walruses, and only 15 pups having been born in captivity in the last eight decades. To discover more about their behaviour she heads from her base in California to their breeding grounds in northern Alaska to see if the answers can be found in the wild

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The group settle into life in their base in Jaipur, India, as Wayne Sleep and Bobby George have a cut-throat shave, Patti Boulaye and Miriam Margoyles visit a local laundrywoman, and Jan Leeming attends a psychic guru and is astounded at his profound observations on her life. To end the second week, the celebrities take a 150-mile trip to the Taj Mahal, some by train and others by chauffeur-driven car, and the sheer beauty of the world's most impressive monument to love leaves Miriam in tears

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV presenter Michelle Ackerley and astrologer Russell Grant team up with experts Roo Irvine and Phil Serrell for an antiques journey on one of the hottest days of the summer. The two teams traverse the Home Counties in search of antiques to sell at auction. Russell is drawn to all things sparkly and at the modern end of the market, while Phil struggles to convince Michelle to buy classic pieces of furniture

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: With the Walmington-on-Sea Home Guard now established, Captain Mainwaring sets about trying to get equipment for the platoon so they can see off potential Nazi invaders. Classic comedy first shown in 1968, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Jack Dee: Sent to Siberia
programme.desc: The dead-pan funnyman travels to Russia's most inhospitable environment, where he journeys through the frozen wastelands accompanied by a herd of reindeer. He begins by learning the basic survival skills needed to set off on the 100km journey, withstanding extreme weather conditions. Armed with just the bare necessities and, of course, his own irrepressible personality, his challenge is to endure the elements and win over the Siberians with his efforts

programme.title: The White Crow (2019)
programme.desc: Rudolf Nureyev travels to Paris to fulfil his dream of becoming a top ballet dancer. However, he faces resistance from KGB officers and ultimately finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, directed by and starring Ralph Fiennes, with Oleg Ivenko and Louis Hofmann

programme.title: Pawn Sacrifice (2014)
programme.desc: American chess prodigy Bobby Fischer challenges the best player in the Soviet Union in a top championship, causing the two nations to use the event to fuel their respective agendas. Fischer soon finds himself caught between the two superpowers as well as having to deal with his own struggles. Tobey Maguire and Liev Schreiber star in this fact-based sporting drama set in 1972 during the Cold War

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Action from today's play at Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the open singles semi-finals

programme.title: The Spy Gone North (2018)
programme.desc: An army major in South Korean military intelligence is assigned a mission to determine whether North Koreans are adhering to their commitment to cease developing nuclear weapons. Spy thriller, starring Jung-min Hwang, Sung-min Lee, Jin-woong Cho and Ji-Hoon Ju. In Korean with English subtitles

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Lee and Debbie Daniels had been through their fair share of problems and thought a new life in New Zealand might be the answer. Five years on, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they decided to make the move

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letters X, Y and Z, concluding her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Oxfordshire. She visits Waterperry, where gardeners are the custodians of a formidable legacy, and chats to Mary Spiller, the first female presenter of Gardeners' World. She also meets the third-generation owner of a remarkable garden at Kingston Bagpuize, and discovers the roots of the Henley Regatta

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap head to Somerset to visit the Mendips to find out how this stunning but fragile area is being protected from the invasion of visitors. Ellie scales Cheddar Gorge as well as discovering the damage cars are wreaking on narrow winding roads, and how white-clawed freshwater crayfish are being saved from voracious invaders. Tom finds out how the countryside can help transform lives and also investigates if the government's new green subsidy scheme can really deliver for farmers. Adam Henson meets ex-international rugby union ref turned Nigel Owens, who is now a farmer and facing his herd's first TB test

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway treks from Harlech to Barmouth, stripping down to basics as he crosses a nudist beach in Gwynedd. On this walk along one of Wales' most popular coastlines, Derek also rolls a cheese down the world's steepest hill and learns about the 'Devil in the Shawl' when he visits an old village chapel. Along his journey, he nearly gets caught out on a tidal causeway and meets a team of women who give up their free time to keep the beaches clean and safe for wildlife

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick journeys inland from the majestic Camel Estuary to one of Britain's finest vineyards, where he joins in harvesting the grapes to discover that the Cornish weather is perfect making some of the best wine in the country. Just off Pentire Point, as the sun sets, Rick rides the gentle swell of the north Atlantic with an old friend, casting his line to hook Britain's fastest fish, before firing up the barbeque to cook his catch with a recipe inspired from a trip to Goa. Rick joins Tim Smit, best known for starting the hugely successful Eden Project, who reveals to Rick his new and ambitious idea in Cornwall

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: The food writer focuses on the best dishes from the series

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams compete at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthen, where presenter Eric Knowles is joined by experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper. The host also chats to ceramic artist Jonathan Cox, whose work is inspired by the Welsh countryside

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Marking the year Songs of Praise turns 60, Aled Jones celebrates at St George's Chapel in Windsor, with Christian stories that exemplify the spirit of the series. Aled and long-serving presenter Pam Rhodes recall past favourite moments including the Queen's celebrated appearance on the programme in 2018, and with the help of archive, the moment Pam and Aled first met on screen in 1995

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the open singles final. Robert Paxton prevailed 7-10, 8-7, 2-1 in 2020, thwarting his opponent Nick Brett's attempt to become the first player in history to win a treble of this event, the open pairs and the mixed pairs in the same year

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team examine antiques at Ashridge House, Hertfordshire. Thomas Plant discovers a marble cupid statue while a 19th-century brass microscope with an extensive collection of botanical slides catches the eye of Claire Rawle. Plus, the presenter takes time out to explore art at the Henry Moore Foundation in Much Hadham, which houses the largest collection of Moore's work

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Action from the prestigious men's World Cup downhill race in Kitzbuhel, Austria, as the skiiers negotiated the infamous slopes of the Streif. Plus, highlights of Europe's biggest snowboarding competition the Laax Open in Switzerland

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about the county's fishing communities, beginning in the popular tourist resort of Newquay as restrictions begin to ease after the coronavirus lockdown. It is not a moment too soon for the town's fishermen, most of whom have been tied up for months, and with lost time to catch up on, and livelihoods on the line, the fleet is steaming into a summer like no other

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve presents highlights of his journeys around the world, and catches up with the people he met, including a Burmese undercover human rights campaigner, a homeless woman living in a Hollywood railway bridge, and a 10 year-old boy he met working in terrible conditions in a glass factory in Bangladesh

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: The last months of Donald Trump's presidency, following his efforts to overthrow the results of the 2020 election and the subsequent violent insurrection against the US Congress. Featuring contributions from advisors and close observers, and a look behind the scenes at the crowded rallies Trump held while the nation was suffering through the Covid-19 pandemic

programme.title: Lance
programme.desc: Part one of two. A personal examination of the rise and fall of Lance Armstrong. The series examines the young Texan cyclist's rise to fame, his struggle with testicular cancer and subsequent emergence as a global icon with seven consecutive Tour de France titles, and his eventual downfall when he was revealed as being at the centre of one of the biggest doping scandals in sporting history

programme.title: The Light Between Oceans (2016)
programme.desc: A childless lighthouse keeper and his wife living off the coast of Western Australia retrieve a baby girl from a drifting rowing boat, found alongside a dead man's body. The shattering consequences of their decision not to report the event and raise the child as their own seem set to change their lives for ever. Drama, starring Michael Fassbender, Alicia Vikander and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: As Nathan continues to hunt down all those with abilities, Matt paints a series of prophetic images that reveal the tragic fate of someone close to him. Sylar encounters an outcast while searching for his father and discovers the stranger has information and an ability of his own

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Homeland Security is instructed by the president to investigate Nathan's nefarious plot, but it may already be too late to save Sylar and Luke from the agents hunting them down. Claire has to protect a young hero from her own father as Noah sets out to capture him, while Matt's prophetic images lead Hiro and Ando into India. Adrian Pasdar, Zachary Quinto and Hayden Panettiere star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Skylar struggles to forgive herself as the hearing reaches a verdict. Jodie's campaign against Beka puts Sacha's life in danger too, and the only hope is that Lucky can work out what is going on before it's too late. While Jac tries to throw herself into her working day, she is unable to shake her concerns about what Kian is getting up to

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion observes which side raises the greater profit and also looks at the history of English jigsaw company Victory Games

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Young farmer Izzi took over from her father aged just 22. She's responsible for a herd of 43 Highland cows and some rare Lincoln Reds. Sisters Laura and Samantha started making award-winning wines on 12 acres of their father's farm four years ago. With lots of overheads and plans for their farms' futures, these women head to the beautiful Creake Abbey Famers' Market to try and fill the coffers. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Christchurch Park in Ipswich, where items assessed include an heirloom Rolex, memorabilia related to Donald Campbell's land-speed record and a necklace worth half a million pounds. There is also a rare Roman relic dug up in the garden and a pocket watch with a hidden secret

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Inside the Human Body
programme.desc: Michael Mosley explores the workings of the human body and tells the stories of people who have pushed their bodies to the absolute limit. He begins at the moment of conception, using state-of-the-art graphics to reveal the sequence of events in the lead-up to a birth. The programme also follows the progress of a couple who are expecting triplets and meets a woman pregnant with her 16th child

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: The antiques challenge sees Paul Hayes and John Cameron searching for items at a car boot sale in Essex to sell for profit, with a budget of £250 each

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary telling how the world's smallest animals manage to survive, revealing how they have evolved extraordinary skills and achieved mindboggling feats. From the animal kingdom's greatest artist to the tiny creatures that provide people with so much of the air they breathe, the programme discovers what it takes to be a miniature miracle. Hugh Dennis narrates

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Tall grasses and beautiful flowers flourish as heavy rains leave the Kalahari covered with lush foliage. Although life is looking good for the Whiskers, the unusual conditions could spell disaster for the family. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where Anita Manning falls in love with a pair of Pilkington vases and Mark Stacey finds an unusual lightbulb. Paul also goes behind the scenes of Capesthorne to find out what it takes to keep a stately home running

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The volunteers are short on time for cooking and exercise, so Tom offers solutions to keep them on track, including quick and delicious recipes, and tips for planning ahead. For the first recipe, Tom makes a pot of beef mince - the starting point for some of the nation's favourite dishes. He uses the batch to make a cottage pie, while the rest is frozen to be used later, perhaps for chilli con carne, spaghetti bolognese or lasagne

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Tchaikovsky, the Evil Dead trilogy, Rafael Nadal, and This Country, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Barons and the Colleagues compete for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the third of the quarter-finals, two teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The documentary follows the Toms as they try to resurrect the last boat built in Looe and invest in the future of the town's fishing fleet, with three generations throwing themselves into the fray. The Toms' journey to get the boat back out and fishing takes them through the challenges of Britain's quota system, in which just five families own nearly a third of the country's entire fish quota

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from October 2019, as comedian and actor Miles Jupp and journalist Camilla Long join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the news

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Confronting Holocaust Denial with David Baddiel
programme.desc: The Holocaust is one of the most documented, witnessed and written about events in history, yet one in six people worldwide either think its scale has been exaggerated or deny that it took place. In this film, comedian David Baddiel explores the multi-faceted nature of Holocaust denial - in both historical and contemporary terms - in an attempt to understand what motivates this phenomenon and why it is on the rise, both in Britain and across the globe

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap head to Somerset to visit the Mendips to find out how this stunning but fragile area is being protected from the invasion of visitors. Ellie scales Cheddar Gorge as well as discovering the damage cars are wreaking on narrow winding roads, and how white-clawed freshwater crayfish are being saved from voracious invaders. Tom finds out how the countryside can help transform lives and also investigates if the government's new green subsidy scheme can really deliver for farmers. Adam Henson meets ex-international rugby union ref turned Nigel Owens, who is now a farmer and facing his herd's first TB test

programme.title: Death in Bollywood
programme.desc: Documentary following the British family of actress Jiah Khan in a search for the truth about her death in Mumbai in 2013, which was ruled a suicide by police. Jiah's mother Rabbiya hired private forensic investigator Jason Payne James, who believes it is highly unlikely that a distinctive oval mark found on the 25-year-old's neck could be caused by the dupatta scarf she was found hanging from in her flat

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A wedding video cracks the case of a teenager's abduction, and an expired pilot's licence exposes a fraudster flying under the radar. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor embarks on the last leg of his journey, passing through the dramatic mountains of the Hoggar Massif, visiting Libya and crossing into Tunisia, where he relives the filming of The Life of Brian. He also visits Algiers and the Spanish enclave of Ceuta before finishing the expedition where he started - Gibraltar

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn relive the golden age of steam. They begin by exploring how the introduction of steam railways in the early 19th century created a domestic revolution, from the way people lived to the food they ate. Arriving at Ffestiniog Railway in Snowdonia, they find out how millions of tons of slate were moved down the mountain, while underground, Alex experiences the brutal conditions face by miners in the Llechwedd Quarry

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure confronts Gilou after he is instrumental in helping Cisco's gang acquire a stash of weapons for the upcoming heist. Meanwhile, Edelman must learn how to best act in Lola's defence. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure tells Ali that Gilou has infiltrated Cisco's gang and they'll need to play for time in their investigation to protect him - but their efforts are jeopardised when Judge Bourdeiou orders the arrest of Cisco's son Titi

programme.title: Paris
programme.desc: Sandrine Voillet investigates the most famous underground movements that defined the city in the 20th century, including existentialism and the legacy of the Moulin Rouge. She also makes a nouvelle vague film, discusses pop music with Jane Birkin and visits the location of the student strikes in May 1968

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on July 5, with performances by Inspiral Carpets, Janet Jackson, Poison, Double Trouble, MC Tunes versus 808 State, FAB featuring MC Parker, Craig McLachlan and Check 1-2, Massivo featuring Tracy, MC Hammer, Elton John and Glenn Medeiros featuring Bobby Brown

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on July 12, with performances by Gun, River City People, Blue Pearl, Glenn Medeiros featuring Bobby Brown, Thunder, the Soup Dragons featuring Junior Reid, the Stone Roses, Elton John and Technotronic featuring Ya Kid K

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Stephen and Gillian have been married for 24 years and been in New Order together for even longer, but they still manage to surprise one another with their musical tastes. While Stephen declares Captain Beefheart an early influence, Gillian confesses her teenage love for a disco classic. Amidst an hour of top tunes, Stephen also reveals the moment he was mistaken for Stevie Wonder and Gillian recalls how her father was a fan of punk. From Kraftwerk to Can, David Bowie to Kate Bush, Magazine to Grace Jones and many more, their stellar playlist is brimming with iconic performances

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor embarks on the last leg of his journey, passing through the dramatic mountains of the Hoggar Massif, visiting Libya and crossing into Tunisia, where he relives the filming of The Life of Brian. He also visits Algiers and the Spanish enclave of Ceuta before finishing the expedition where he started - Gibraltar

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn relive the golden age of steam. They begin by exploring how the introduction of steam railways in the early 19th century created a domestic revolution, from the way people lived to the food they ate. Arriving at Ffestiniog Railway in Snowdonia, they find out how millions of tons of slate were moved down the mountain, while underground, Alex experiences the brutal conditions face by miners in the Llechwedd Quarry

programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank
programme.desc: Drama based on the famous diaries of the Jewish girl who went into hiding with her family in Nazi-occupied Amsterdam after her 16-year-old sister Margot received orders to attend a labour camp in 1942. Starring Ellie Kendrick, Iain Glen, Tamsin Greig, Felicity Jones and Lesley Sharp

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: The presenter tells the story of the 17th-century residence in Derbyshire - the pleasure palace of playboy Cavalier and ambitious courtier William Cavendish. Through a collection of artworks and items held within the castle, Lucy uncovers a colourful tale, capturing the tensions of England in the 1600s that would eventually lead the nation to civil war

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1880s, when unemployment was high and living conditions intolerable, but people desperate for work still arrived in London. The Howarths find themselves employing new workers in their Victorian sweat shop, while the Potters become street vendors, selling sheep's trotters and jellied eels in the East End. Michael Mosley presents

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: Charley Boorman applies his new-found sailing skills as he crosses the English Channel in a dinghy. His journey then takes him through mainland Europe and western Asia using a variety of vehicles, in a race to make it to Dubai in time to catch a container ship to Mumbai, India. However, he must first negotiate a host of obstacles, including the bureaucratic hurdle of being granted the necessary visas to enter Iran

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about two members of a family who reappear 40 years after they were taken away from their mother as toddlers, having been told by their father she did not want them. Lillian was a teenage divorcee in 1950s London and her ex-husband took their children Michelle and Andrew to the local park on his visiting day and they never returned. As they grew up and saw their father's unorthodox relationship with other women, Michelle and Andrew realised their mother may have wanted them after all, so a 20-year search to find her began

programme.title: Victorian Sensations
programme.desc: In the final episode, psychotherapist Philippa Perry explores ghosts, science and early cinema in the 1890s, and how the late Victorian passion for science co-exists with a deeply-held belief in the paranormal

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore uncovers the role of religion in creating and maintaining the power of the city. In the first episode, he explores how every event in ancient Rome revolved around worship and that nothing took place without calling on the Pantheon, the temple to their gods. He also reveals the part played by sacredness and sacrifice in Roman culture

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1880s, when unemployment was high and living conditions intolerable, but people desperate for work still arrived in London. The Howarths find themselves employing new workers in their Victorian sweat shop, while the Potters become street vendors, selling sheep's trotters and jellied eels in the East End. programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank
programme.desc: The families get used to their incarceration and adapt to the strict rules of daily quiet by which they must live. Anne finds Mrs van Daan increasingly hard to bear and another Jewish man, Mr Dussel, joins them in hiding and becomes the butt of all the teenager's jokes and frustrations

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene depicting a beautiful snowy day outside through a windowpane

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Victoria & Albert curators Steph and Jenny plan a major new exhibition celebrating the work of British fashion designer Mary Quant. A Yorkshire woman donates a 1960s minidress that would be perfect - but it needs a lot of restoration work to make it exhibition-ready. Curator Julius uncovers some of the first objects ever given to the museum, including a hand-painted writing case that once belonged to Queen Victoria, while Corinna hits the streets to gather the most significant designs of today - the flags and logo of climate change activists Extinction Rebellion

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines the qualities necessary for a film to become a cult classic, as well as the main types of a genre that is dictated by the audience rather than the film-maker. Marks reveals how some set out to shock and break taboos, while others are camp classics embraced by viewers who find new or hidden meanings in them

programme.title: Score: Cinema's Greatest Soundtracks
programme.desc: Hollywood composers including Hans Zimmer, James Cameron, Danny Elfman, John Williams, Quincy Jones and Trent Reznor combine to give a privileged look inside the musical challenges and creative secrecy of the film score

programme.title: Australian Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: A look at a wide-ranging selection of songs and music acts that came from Down Under, with clips of their appearances on some top UK shows. Featured chartbusters include pop pioneers the Easybeats, rock 'n' roll legends AC/DC, and INXS who became one of the nation's most successful acts of all time. Also included are ground-breaking Aborigine band Yothu Yindi, and the more familiar faces of Nick Cave, John Farnham, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: When Lily is upset to realise Matt is going out with cool girl Greta Masters, Jas plans a make-over slumber party to cheer her up. Unfortunately it clashes with a rare comet sighting, which Martha and Ollie are desperate to experience. They have even been allowed to borrow the very precious school telescope for the evening. Ollie is smuggled in to Lily's house by Martha to watch secretly from the attic, with Martha planning to split her time between both events. After much chaos and confusion, and with the presence of Ollie in the house rumbled, a compromise is finally reached, where the girls all get to do both the make-over and watch the rare comet. At the end of the make-over session Martha and Jas are horrified to see Lily has transformed herself to look exactly like Greta in a bid to impress Matt. programme.desc: In this programme we visit three children who are all having a fascinating day out. In Russia, seven-year-old Liza picks blueberries with her grandma - and helps turn them into delicious pancakes. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, goes to the market with her mum and learns how to do the shopping. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and discoveries of childhood are universal.

programme.desc: Explore a mosaic lion and the disco-dance of knights

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we meet a new family, five-year-old twins Daisy and Lily. They live near the seaside with their mum, dad and younger twin sisters Molly and Nell. The family has a busy breakfast time with all those mouths to feed, and Daisy and Lily are in charge of counting the eggs. Later, Mum takes the girls out for some fun on the beach where they make a picture using pebbles and seaweed. Back at home Daisy and Lily get to work in their toy kitchen, making pretend soup for all the family. But will their sausage roll and vegetable soup be a success at their super-soup party?

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig's parents have a very busy day - and she cannot understand why they have not paid her any attention

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: The Olobobs invent a new ball game, but can't agree on the rules. When Lemon accidentally bursts the ball they make Burby, and learn that rules can be invented too

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Essam family members visit the Brooklyn-Bells. Jess hopes their well-behaved dog, Baxter, will teach Waffle how to be good, but Waffle has other ideas

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Mittens loses the new bell on her collar and Timmy helps her find it

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly dresses up as a fairy godmother and makes Suki and Ruby's wishes come true. But Magnus's wish to build a shiny robot doesn't go as smoothly

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Giraffe gets her head stuck in a honey tree, and as it grows it stretches her neck until she is able to reach the highest branches

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee cannot stop playing his favourite game on Mo's tablet, so Mo tries to convince him that it's just as much fun playing outside

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Drummer D has his drumsticks but nothing to play on, so his friends help him find something he can use to deliver a dazzling solo

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Three does magic tricks with apples to show the others who goes first, who is biggest and how to surprise number friends

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny is heartbroken when his favourite book falls into the bath and gets wet, but Flop helps him understand that the story can still be told

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Science-educational series following a curious, adventure-seeking and unruly monster as he finds the answers to everything a pre-school child would want to know

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend pretend to be superheroes for the day. Meanwhile, Super Tumble goes to the rescue of Shopkeeper Tumble, Baker Tumble and Aunt Polly

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies say 'Eh-oh' to one another in turn and then to the flowers, while the children in Tummy Tales make a friendship circle out of handprints

programme.title: Mister Maker at Home
programme.desc: Mister Maker is having trouble using his trusty Makerphone and there’s only one friend who will be best placed to help him. He video-calls his friend Jen from CBeebies’s Andy’s Aquatic Adventures, which leads to arty inspiration! Mister Maker makes a pretend computer out of lots of card, stickers, fun foam and buttons. Later, Mister Maker checks in on his friends, The Shapes. They are snoring away, so Mister M makes sure he leaves them to have a nice rest. Mister Maker then decides to dance like a Shape himself, which in turn inspires him to make some shape art - he turns an old sock into something special! Lastly, Mister Maker remembers all the fun he has had throughout the day.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: One of his workers turns out to be a cat who makes fishy dog biscuits. Dog helps the owner by showing him that he can make money by producing both cat snacks and doggy biscuits.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne visits Marlee, who is taking her pet dog Missy to the vet. Rory learns how the vet at a zoo keeps flies from bothering some very special rhinos

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: It is an exciting day for five-year-old twins Daisy and Lily. Mum and Dad are bringing home their new family pet - Foxy the puppy

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing steam machines. She drives a paddle steamer around a river, shows off the super-fast Inspiration land speed car and then drives an old steam car

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook and Line try to convince Captain Captain to play her concertina. Gem's Swashbucklers Daisy, Mackenzie, Molly and Aaron play Tumbly Barrel and Swashbubbles

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When the Squirrel Tribe forget his birthday, Nutkin quits the squirrel life and decides to become a rabbit

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Searching in North America for a Stegosaurus - a plant eater that was as long as a double decker bus. Presented by Andy Day

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: It's the Snowy Goat Gala on High Snowy Mountain and Little Bo Purl is meant to be performing her famous snowy yodelling

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters master the art of mindfulness to save the Giant Buddha of Leshan in China

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly dresses up as a fairy godmother and makes Suki and Ruby's wishes come true. But Magnus's wish to build a shiny robot doesn't go as smoothly

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran go ice skating. Whilst Gran Gran is a little nervous JoJo is a natural. With Jared’s help Gran Gran soon gets the hang of it!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The local park is closed so Sarah and Duck search for a suitable alternative

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Mr Onion looks inside a book and decides to tell a story to his best friend Dibillo

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small find a strange silver ball that glows and rings when it bounces. Mother tells them it is a living thing, and Granny is convinced it must have friends nearby

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Tombliboo Eee does not know what to do - until she decides to play the piano

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets his good friend, the actor and comedian Barry Humphries, who fell in love with Cornwall in the 1960s when he escaped London to develop his famous character Dame Edna Everage. Barry talks about his friendship with British poet John Betjeman, and asks Rick to review his home-made fishcakes. Rick also goes fishing for crab, before cooking a simple yet delicious crab omelette. The host then takes a dip in the chilly north Atlantic with an eccentric group of wild water swimmers called the Perranporth Blue Tits

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
